brief_justification: Correctly identifies B with valid structural mapping across all analogy components; constructs a multi-level isomorphism (algorithm→process, perceptual model→cultural salience, irreversibility→forgetting, structure→narrative arc) going well beyond surface identification; proceeds linearly without naming potential biases or questioning whether the analogy itself might be underdetermined; verifies by falsifying all three alternatives with specific structural reasons (super-resolution, lossless, adversarial manipulation), functioning as counterfactual checks; presents the structural mapping as certain without acknowledging that 'collective memory' is itself a contested theoretical construct or that the analogy has limits; systematically evaluates A, C, and D with distinct rejection reasons tied to specific properties of the compression model; every conclusion traces to an explicit derivation step in the structural mapping, fully reproducible by a third party.
